verses.text: God asks where the bill of His people's divorce is, for they were sold only by their own sins; is His hand 'shortened, that it cannot redeem?' He did not cast them off. Their estrangement is their own doing, not God's failure or weakness.

God asks where the bill of His people's divorce is, for they were sold only by their own sins; is His hand 'shortened, that it cannot redeem?' He did not cast them off. Their estrangement is their own doing, not God's failure or weakness.

God asks where the bill of His people's divorce is, for they were sold only by their own sins; is His hand 'shortened, that it cannot redeem?' He did not cast them off. Their estrangement is their own doing, not God's failure or weakness.

The Servant says God gave him 'the tongue of the learned' to comfort the weary and wakens his ear morning by morning; 'I was not rebellious.' Christ learned obedience. The willing Servant listens daily and never turns back.

The Servant says God gave him 'the tongue of the learned' to comfort the weary and wakens his ear morning by morning; 'I was not rebellious.' Christ learned obedience. The willing Servant listens daily and never turns back.

'I gave my back to the smiters... I hid not my face from shame and spitting,' for the Lord GOD helps him. This foretells Christ's passion. The Servant bore mockery and blows with set, trusting resolve.

'I gave my back to the smiters... I hid not my face from shame and spitting,' for the Lord GOD helps him. This foretells Christ's passion. The Servant bore mockery and blows with set, trusting resolve.

'He is near that justifieth me; who will contend with me?' for God will help, and his accusers shall wear out like a garment. Vindication is sure. The one God justifies has nothing to fear from any accuser.

'He is near that justifieth me; who will contend with me?' for God will help, and his accusers shall wear out like a garment. Vindication is sure. The one God justifies has nothing to fear from any accuser.

Who walks in darkness with no light? 'Let him trust in the name of the LORD'; but those who kindle their own fire 'shall lie down in sorrow.' Faith waits on God. To make our own light instead of trusting Him ends in grief.
